CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID is updated, and a row is added to this table.   
The third column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the reason for updating the document. Column 5 should include the names of the author(s) 
doing the changes. Including the name in column 6 shows that the document has been validated by the 
TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation means the 
document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
 
Level-2 Pre -processed 
(L2P) data product 
Geophysical variables derived from Level 1 source data on the Level 1 
grid (typically the satellite swath projection). They are ancillary data and 
metadata added following GHRSST Data Specification. 
Level-3 Uncollated (L3U) 
data product 
L2 data granules remapped to a regular latitude/longitude grid without 
combining observations from multiple source files. L3U files will typically 
be “sparse”, corresponding to a single satellite orbit. 
Level-3 Collated (L3C) 
data product 
SST observations from a single instrument combined into a space -time 
grid. In this project, a typical L3C file may contain all the observations 
from a single instrument in a 24-hour period. 
Level-3 Super -Collated 
(L3S) data product 
SST observations from many instruments combined into a space -time 
grid. In this project, a typical L3S file may contain all the observations 
from all available instruments in a 24-hour period. 
Level 4 (L4) data product SST observations from multiple instruments using an analysis system 
(e.g. optimal interpolation) to produce a gridded, gap-free product. 
Sea Ice Corresponds to ocean surface covered by sea ice (>70%) thus the surface 
temperature is classified as sea-ice surface temperature (IST). 
Sea Ice Concentration Fraction (percentage) of the sea surface area of the grid cell 
containing sea-ice. 
Sea Surface Temperature 
(SST) 
SST is the temperature of the ocean water close to the surface. 
Sea Surface Temperature 
at the base of the thermal 
layer (SSTfoundation) 
The sea surface foundation temperature ( SSTfoundation) represents the 
average temperature of the upper ocean layer, free from diurnal (daily) 
fluctuations, such as those caused by solar heating. 
Sea Surface Temperature 
at the base of the skin 
layer (SSTsub-skin) 
The sea surface subskin temperature (SST sub-skin) is the sea surface 
temperature at the base of the ocean's "skin layer," a very thin (less than 
1 mm) layer where heat transfer is dominated by conduction, not 
turbulence. 
Thermal skin layer The layer within which the long -wave radiation penetrates the ocean, 
typically less than 1mm, where ocean-atmosphere heat exchange takes 
place and temperature is measured using Infra-red sensors.
